title: Pre-Qualification Requirements and Licensing Procedures for Manning Agencies
content:
Section 4 outlines the pre-qualification requirements for any person applying for a license to operate a manning agency, including submission of business registration documents, proof of financial capacity, proof of existence of new principal, educational or experience qualifications, valid clearances, notarized undertakings regarding responsibilities and liabilities, lists of officials and personnel, attendance certificates for orientation seminars, recruitment procedure flowcharts, and a four-year business plan.

Section 5 requires payment of a non-refundable filing fee of PhP25,000 upon receipt of complete application requirements.

Section 6 mandates a panel interview for proprietors, partners, and board members to assess qualifications.

Section 7 requires the Administration to notify applicants of the decision within three working days after the panel interview.

Section 8 states grounds for denial of application include failure to meet requirements or submission of false information.

Section 9 requires submission of post-qualification requirements prior to issuance of a provisional license, including office lease or ownership proof, office layout, inventory of office equipment, organizational chart, payment of license fee of PhP100,000, and an escrow agreement with a bank with a deposit of PhP1,000,000 to cover claims and compliance guarantees.

Section 10 mandates an assessment by the Administration of compliance with office space and equipment requirements prior to license issuance, conducted by the Labor Laws Compliance Officer according to Rule III provisions.
